title: Dioscorea lloydiana
intro: Dioscorea lloydiana, also known as the Lloyd's yam or mountain yam, is a species of flowering plant in the yam family Dioscoreaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central America. The plant has been found to have numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Dioscorea lloydiana include diosgenin, a steroidal saponin that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, as well as other bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. Dioscorea lloydiana is commonly used in herbal medicine and can be found in various preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, as well as in topical creams and ointments to support skin health and wound healing.
taxonomy: Dioscorea lloydiana is a member of the plant family Dioscoreaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as yams. Dioscoreaceae is a small family of monocotyledonous plants, and Dioscorea lloydiana is one of the species within this family. The genus Dioscorea is a large and diverse group of plants, comprising around 600 to 700 species. Dioscorea lloydiana is native to the Andes mountain range in South America.
